---
title: "Mithun Diet Analysis"
output: html_notebook
---
```{r}
read in the data
diet.physeq<-read_rds("diet.physeq.rds")
```

```{r Figure 2B}
# Load required libraries
library(phyloseq)
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)

# Step 1: Aggregate at family level

diet.physeq.fam <- tax_glom(diet.physeq, "family_name")

# Step 2: Transform to relative abundance
diet.physeq.fam.rel <- transform_sample_counts(diet.physeq.fam, function(x) x / sum(x))

# Step 3: Melt to long format
diet.physeq_melted <- psmelt(diet.physeq.fam.rel)

# Step 4: Collapse low abundance families (<1%) into "Other"
family_abundance <- diet.physeq_melted %>%
  group_by(family_name) %>%
  summarise(TotalAbundance = sum(Abundance)) %>%
  mutate(RelativeAbundance = TotalAbundance / sum(TotalAbundance),
         family_name_clean = ifelse(RelativeAbundance < 0.01, "Other", as.character(family_name)))

# Merge collapsed family names back into melted data
diet.physeq_melted <- dplyr::left_join(
  diet.physeq_melted,
  family_abundance[, c("family_name", "family_name_clean")],
  by = "family_name"
)

# Replace family_name with cleaned version for plotting
diet.physeq_melted$family_name <- diet.physeq_melted$family_name_clean

# Step 5: Reorder Season
diet.physeq_melted$Season <- factor(
  diet.physeq_melted$Season,
  levels = c("Spring", "Summer", "Autumn", "Winter")
)

# Step 6: Reorder family_name by total abundance, with "Other" and "NS_family" at the bottom
ordered_families <- diet.physeq_melted %>%
  group_by(family_name) %>%
  summarise(TotalAbundance = sum(Abundance)) %>%
  arrange(desc(TotalAbundance)) %>%
  pull(family_name)

# Move Other and NS_family to the end
ordered_families <- ordered_families[!ordered_families %in% c("NS_family","Other")]
ordered_families <- c(ordered_families, "NS_family","Other")

# Apply the ordering
diet.physeq_melted$family_name <- factor(diet.physeq_melted$family_name, levels = ordered_families)

# Step 7: Create color map
family_names <- levels(diet.physeq_melted$family_name)
n_families <- length(family_names)

# Define up to 24 colors + gray fallback
custom_colors <- c(
  "#66c2a5", "#fc8d62", "#8da0bb", "#e78ac3", "#a6d854", "#ffd92f",
  "#e5c494", "#1b9e77", "#d95f02", "#7570b9", "#e7298a", "#a6761d",
  "#666666", "#1f78b4", "#b2df8a", "#cab2d6", "#fdbf6f", "#ff7f00",
  "#fb9a99", "#6a3d9a", "#b15928", "#bc80bd", "#ccebc5", "#999999"
)

# Assign colors to family names
color_map <- setNames(custom_colors[1:n_families], family_names)

# Force "Other" and "NS_family" to gray
color_map["Other"] <- "#666666"
color_map["NS_family"] <- "gray"

# Step 8: Plot
ggplot(diet.physeq_melted, aes(x = Sample, y = Abundance, fill = family_name)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "stack") +
  facet_grid(~Season, scales = "free") +
  theme_bw() +
  labs(x = "Sample", y = "Relative Abundance") +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, hjust = 1),
        legend.position = "top") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = color_map)


```
```{r Figure 2D}
# Add sample metadata

metadata
diet.physeq_diet.rich <- estimate_richness(diet.physeq, measures = c("Observed", "Chao1", "Shannon", "Simpson"))


diet.physeq_diet.rich$SampleID.Original <- rownames(diet.physeq_diet.rich )

diet.physeq_diet.rich  <- merge(diet.physeq_diet.rich ,metadata, by = "SampleID.Original")

diet.physeq_diet.rich.long <- melt(diet.physeq_diet.rich, id.vars = c("Species", "Season","SampleID","Ind","SampleID.Original"))
diet.physeq_diet.rich.long

diet.physeq_diet.rich.long.obs<- diet.physeq_diet.rich.long %>% filter(variable=="Observed")

shapiro.test(as.numeric(diet.physeq_diet.rich.long.obs$value))
diet.physeq_diet.rich.long.shannon<- diet.physeq_diet.rich.long %>% filter(variable=="Shannon")

shapiro.test(as.numeric(diet.physeq_diet.rich.long.obs$value))



aov_shan <-  aov(value ~ Season + Error(Ind), data = diet.physeq_diet.rich.long.shannon)
summary(aov_shan )

aov_obs.diet <- aov(value ~ Season + Error(Ind), data = diet.physeq_diet.rich.long.obs)
summary(aov_obs.diet)

  aov_shan.diet <- aov(as.numeric(value) ~ Season + Error(Ind), data = diet.physeq_diet.rich.long.shannon)
summary(aov_shan.diet)

 aov(as.numeric(value) ~ Season + Error(Ind), data = diet.physeq_diet.rich.long.shannon)
summary(aov_shan.diet)


diet.physeq_diet.rich.long.shannon$value<-as.numeric(diet.physeq_diet.rich.long.shannon$value)

  
  
diet.physeq_diet.rich.long.shannon %>%
  ggboxplot(
    x = "Season",
    y = "value",
    fill = "Season",
    add = "jitter",
    add.params = list(size = 1.8, alpha = 0.5)
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c(
    "Spring" = "#009E73", 
    "Summer" = "#F0E442",
    "Autumn" = "#E69F00", 
    "Winter" = "#56B4E9"
  ))

```

```{r Figure 3B}

diet.physeq.aitchison <- microbiome::transform(diet.physeq.filtered, "clr")
#diet.physeq.aitchison %>% write_rds("~/Desktop/diet.physeq.aitchison.rds")

# Perform PCoA
pcoa_results <- ordinate(diet.physeq.aitchison, method = "PCoA", distance = "euclidean")
# Convert PCoA results into a dataframe
pcoa_df <- as.data.frame(pcoa_results$vectors[, 1:2])  # First 2 components
colnames(pcoa_df) <- c("PCoA1", "PCoA2")

# Add sample metadata
metadata <- as(sample_data(diet.physeq), "data.frame")
pcoa_df$SampleID.Original <- rownames(pcoa_df)
pcoa_df <- merge(pcoa_df, metadata, by = "SampleID.Original")


# Ensure Season order is: Spring → Summer → Autumn → Winter
pcoa_df$Season <- factor(pcoa_df$Season, levels = c("Spring", "Summer", "Autumn", "Winter"))

ggplot(pcoa_df, aes(x = PCoA1, y = -PCoA2, color = Season, group = Ind)) +
  geom_path(aes(group = Ind,linetype =Ind), color = "gray50", alpha = 0.5, size = 0.7, arrow = arrow(type = "closed", length = unit(0.1, "inches"))) +
  geom_point(size = 4, alpha = 0.9, stroke = 0.8, shape = 21, fill = NA) +
  stat_ellipse(aes(group = Season, fill = Season), alpha = 0.2, geom = "polygon", color = NA) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  coord_fixed(ratio = 1, clip = "off")+
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Spring" = "#009E73", "Summer" = "#F0E442", 
                                "Autumn" = "#E69F00", "Winter" = "#56B4E9")) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Spring" = "#009E73", "Summer" = "#F0E442", 
                                "Autumn" = "#E69F00", "Winter" = "#56B4E9"))+labs(title = "PCoA of Aitchison-transformed Data ",
       x = paste0("PCoA1 (", round(pcoa_results$values$Relative_eig[1] * 100, 2), "%)"),
       y = paste0("PCoA2 (", round(pcoa_results$values$Relative_eig[2] * 100, 2), "%)")) 

```

```{r Figure 3D}
library(microViz)
library(phyloseq)
library(ggplot2)

# Get current tax_table and replace rownames with Family_name
physeq_diet.clr<-diet.physeq.filtered

# Step 1: Extract OTU and tax tables
otu_mat <- as(otu_table(physeq_diet.clr), "matrix")
tax_mat <- as(tax_table(physeq_diet.clr), "matrix")

# Step 2: Create Family-based names
tax_df <- as.data.frame(tax_mat)
new_taxa_names <- ifelse(
  !is.na(tax_df$family_name),
  paste(tax_df$family_name,tax_df$genus_name, rownames(tax_df), sep = "_"),
  paste("UnknownFamily", rownames(tax_df), sep = "_")
)

# Step 3: Assign new names to both OTU and tax matrices
rownames(otu_mat) <- new_taxa_names
rownames(tax_mat) <- new_taxa_names

# Step 4: Rebuild the phyloseq object with synced components
physeq_mithun.diet.labeled <- phyloseq(
  otu_table(otu_mat, taxa_are_rows = TRUE),
  tax_table(tax_mat),
  sample_data(physeq_diet.clr)  # include your metadata
)

#Plotting 

# Step 1: Fix tax table and transform
physeq_diet.clr <- physeq_mithun.diet.labeled%>%
  tax_fix(unknowns = c("NS_genus", "NS_family", "family_name")) %>% tax_agg("family_name")  %>%
  tax_transform("clr") %>%
  ord_calc(method = "PCA")

# Step 2: Plot PCA 
pca_plot <- physeq_diet.clr %>%
  ord_plot(
    plot_taxa = 1:15,
    colour = "Season",
    size = 1,
    tax_lab_style = tax_lab_style(max_angle = 70, aspect_ratio = 0.7, repel=TRUE),
    auto_caption = 8
  )


# Step 3: Customize the plot
pca_plot +
  stat_ellipse(aes(linetype = Season, colour = Season), type="euclid",linewidth = 0.3,, level = 0.95) +
  scale_colour_brewer(palette = "Set1") +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom") +
  coord_fixed(ratio = 1, clip = "off")+
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Spring" = "#009E73", "Summer" = "#F0E442", 
                                "Autumn" = "#E69F00", "Winter" = "#56B4E9")) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Spring" = "#009E73", "Summer" = "#F0E442", 
                                "Autumn" = "#E69F00", "Winter" = "#56B4E9"))

```
